D1.name: Agentic Engineering
D1.question: How engineers build
D1.description: Engineers evolve from writing code to orchestrating AI agents that write code. Compresses build time 2-10x. Requires AI-native architecture and pipeline. Bottleneck shifts from engineering capacity to product discovery/shaping.

D2.name: AI in the Product
D2.question: What you build
D2.description: Static forms/dashboards evolve into conversational interfaces. On-demand generated UIs. Embedded agents that act on behalf of users.

D3.name: Build for Agents
D3.question: Who consumes your product
D3.description: Products serve not just humans and traditional APIs, but AI agents as first-class consumers. Emerging interoperability protocols (MCP, A2A). B2A (business-to-agent) joins B2B.

D4.name: Performance & Cost at Scale
D4.question: How you sustain
D4.description: Agent-driven traffic amplifies system load 10-100x. Right-sized, hardware-sympathetic architecture on modern infrastructure. 10x scale at fraction of cost vs over-engineered cloud.

SCE.name: Software Civil Engineering
SCE.question: From craft to discipline
SCE.description: Agentic AI is the forcing function for software's professionalization. Event Modeling provides the specification language (blueprints). Decider pattern enables simulation (terraform plan for domain logic). Lifecycle: Specify → Plan → Verify → Apply → Observe. Six pillars: formal spec, material datasheets, codes/norms, simulation, licensure, education. Spec-Driven Development relocates human judgment to a higher control plane. Human in the loop → human on the loop = 10% → 10× transition.
